data.narrative:
Eikon Therapeutics, a Series B biotech company founded by Luke Lavis, Eric Betzig, Robert Tjian, and Xavier Darzacq, carries a current post-money valuation of $3.3B following $700M+ in total fundraising. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 3 public and 8 private companies in Biotech & Health, with particular weight on 4 same-sector, same-stage peers: Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier to the $3.3B last post-money, implying $6.3B. Peer-median valuation across the 4 same-stage comparables yields $805M, with a range of $600M to $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring using P/S multiples (5.8x median across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure.

The two-method triangulation produces a median value of $3.5B, with low and high bounds of $684M and $7.2B respectively. The current $3.3B valuation sits near the median but reflects substantial dispersion between peer-median ($805M) and stage-progression ($6.3B) approaches. Key uncertainty stems from the peer-median method, which reflects earlier-stage or lower-risk biotech profiles; Eikon's founder composition and capital deployment may justify a premium. Revenue and clinical-stage advancement would resolve valuation ambiguity.
